name: ultraproxy
description: Route Anthropic/OpenAI traffic from a Remote-SSH cluster through a local Xray (ss/ssconf/vless) proxy over a reverse SSH tunnel. Keeps local-network (vLLM) traffic direct.

ultraproxy.shareLink.markdownDescription: Proxy share link: `ss://`, `ssconf://`, `vless://`, or an `http(s)://` subscription URL. For secrecy you can leave this empty and store it via **UltraProxy: Set SSH / Proxy Credentials** instead.

ultraproxy.subscriptionServerName.description: When the share link is a SIP008/subscription with multiple servers, pick the one whose remark/id matches this. Empty = first server.

ultraproxy.whitelistExtras.markdownDescription: Extra destinations that should egress through the proxy (everything else is blocked). Bare hostnames become `domain:<host>`; `domain:`, `full:`, `regexp:`, `geosite:`, `geoip:` prefixes pass through verbatim.

ultraproxy.vllmHost.markdownDescription: Usually **not needed**: vLLM on a private LAN IP (10.x / 172.16-31.x / 192.168.x / localhost) is already excluded by the default `NO_PROXY`, and non-whitelisted traffic goes direct anyway. Only add a host here if your vLLM is reached by a **hostname** that resolves to a private IP (NO_PROXY matches by name, not resolved IP), or if you enabled `strictWhitelist`.

ultraproxy.noProxy.markdownDescription: Hosts kept OFF the proxy (the cluster reaches them on its own internet). Includes localhost, private ranges, and common package/registry hosts so big `pip`/`hf`/`conda` downloads don't tunnel through your local machine. `vllmHost` entries are merged in automatically.

ultraproxy.strictWhitelist.markdownDescription: When **off** (default), traffic that is not on the proxy whitelist is sent `direct` via your local machine, so nothing on the cluster breaks. When **on**, non-whitelisted traffic is **blocked** (strict isolation) — may break `pip`/`git`/etc. run from a proxied shell unless added to `noProxy`.

ultraproxy.allowInsecureTls.description: Honor allowInsecure=1 from the share link (disables proxy-server TLS certificate verification). Off by default so a fetched subscription cannot silently downgrade TLS.

ultraproxy.allowUnverifiedBinary.description: Allow running a downloaded Xray binary whose SHA-256 could not be verified against the release .dgst. Off by default (fail closed).

ultraproxy.clusters.markdownDescription: Multiple clusters to proxy **simultaneously** (one shared local Xray, one reverse tunnel each). When empty, the flat `sshHost`/`sshUser`/… below act as a single cluster. Store each cluster's SSH password/passphrase via **Set Credentials**.

privateKeyPath.description: Private key path for this cluster.

remoteProxyPort.description: Cluster loopback port. Omit to inherit ultraproxy.remoteProxyPort; an explicit 0 forces auto-assign for THIS cluster, overriding the global.

vllmHost.description: Extra local-network hosts to keep off the proxy for THIS cluster.

patchCopilotSettings.description: Patch remote VSCode http.proxy on this cluster.

injectTerminalEnv.description: Inject terminal env on this cluster.

injectServerEnv.description: Write the proxy env into ~/.vscode-server/server-env-setup on this cluster (reaches the extension-host process tree).

wrapClaudeCode.description: Wrap the Claude Code bundled 'claude' binary on this cluster so it inherits the proxy env.

proxyJump.description: Bastion/jump host for this cluster ([user@]host[:port]), like ssh -J. Uses the same credentials as the cluster.

execProfile.description: How remote commands run on this cluster: direct, inside a docker container, or via a custom wrapper.

dockerContainer.description: Docker container name/ID to exec into (when execProfile is 'docker').

execTemplate.description: Custom command wrapper with a {{CMD}} placeholder (when execProfile is 'custom'), e.g. 'sudo {{CMD}}'.

ultraproxy.sshHost.description: Single-cluster fallback SSH host (used when ultraproxy.clusters is empty).

enumDescriptions: Use the running SSH agent (OpenSSH agent / Pageant).
Use a private key file (path below).
Use a password (stored securely via Set Credentials).
ultraproxy.sshAuthMethod.description: Preferred SSH auth method. UltraProxy still falls back through agent -> key -> password -> keyboard-interactive.

ultraproxy.sshPrivateKeyPath.description: Path to an OpenSSH/PEM private key file (read locally). Passphrase, if any, is stored via Set Credentials.

ultraproxy.sshAgentPath.description: Override SSH agent socket/pipe. Empty = auto (\\.\pipe\openssh-ssh-agent on Windows, $SSH_AUTH_SOCK elsewhere).

ultraproxy.remoteProxyPort.markdownDescription: Cluster loopback port the reverse tunnel binds to. `0` = auto-assign — safe on shared hosts, but the port **changes on every reconnect**, which breaks anything that expects a stable proxy address. Use a **fixed** port (e.g. `1081`) with `injectServerEnv` (server-env-setup is only sourced at VS Code Server startup) or when the cluster env hardcodes the proxy URL.

ultraproxy.localSocksPort.description: Local SOCKS5 port for Xray. 0 = auto free port.

ultraproxy.localHttpPort.description: Local HTTP proxy port for Xray (this is what the tunnel targets). 0 = auto free port.

ultraproxy.xrayPath.description: Path to an existing xray/xray.exe binary, or a v2rayN install root (bin/xray/xray.exe is probed). Empty = download the pinned version on first run.

ultraproxy.xrayVersion.description: Pinned XTLS/Xray-core release tag (CalVer, e.g. v26.3.27).

ultraproxy.patchCopilotSettings.description: Write http.proxy/http.noProxy into the remote VSCode Machine settings so GitHub Copilot and other extensions use the tunnel. Requires python3 on the cluster.

ultraproxy.injectTerminalEnv.description: Also write terminal.integrated.env.linux so integrated-terminal tools (Claude Code CLI, SDKs) inherit the proxy env.

ultraproxy.injectServerEnv.markdownDescription: Write the proxy env into `~/.vscode-server/server-env-setup` (a script VS Code Server sources on startup) so `HTTPS_PROXY` reaches the **extension-host process tree** — i.e. subprocesses that extensions spawn (including the Claude Code extension), which the terminal-only env does not cover. Off by default; **requires _Remote-SSH: Kill VS Code Server on Host_ + reconnect to take effect**. Fully reverted on Remove.

ultraproxy.wrapClaudeCode.markdownDescription: Wrap the Anthropic Claude Code extension's bundled native `claude` binary on the cluster with a shim that re-exports the proxy env before exec'ing the real binary, so it traverses the tunnel even when the extension host spawns it with a stripped environment. Invasive (edits another extension's files) and reverted on Remove; Linux/macOS clusters only. **Note:** a Claude Code auto-update replaces the binary and drops the wrap — re-run Apply after updating it.

ultraproxy.sshProxyJump.markdownDescription: Default bastion/jump host for single-cluster mode, in `[user@]host[:port]` form (equivalent to `ssh -J`). The tunnel first connects to this host, then forwards to the cluster through it. The jump uses the **same** credentials (agent/key/password) as the cluster. Per-cluster `clusters[].proxyJump` overrides this.

ultraproxy.execProfile.markdownDescription: How remote injection commands run on the cluster. `direct` runs them on the SSH host (default). `docker` wraps each in `docker exec -i <container> …` so injection targets a container's `$HOME`/`.vscode-server`. `custom` uses `execTemplate`. **Note (docker/custom):** the reverse-tunnel port binds the SSH host's loopback, so the container must share the host network (e.g. `--network host`, or Enroot/Pyxis which do by default) for `127.0.0.1:<port>` to be reachable inside it.

ultraproxy.dockerContainer.description: Docker container name or ID to exec into when execProfile is 'docker'.

ultraproxy.execTemplate.markdownDescription: Custom remote-command wrapper used when `execProfile` is `custom`. Must contain a `{{CMD}}` placeholder, e.g. `sudo {{CMD}}` or `srun --pty {{CMD}}`.

ultraproxy.autoStart.description: Automatically apply the proxy when VSCode starts.

ultraproxy.testEndpoints.markdownDescription: Extra HTTPS URLs to probe in **Test Connection** (in addition to the Anthropic/OpenAI `/v1/models` endpoints). Reachability only — no model listing.

scripts.package: npm run compile && npm run download-xray && vsce package
